region_of_origin: Canada (developed from Brassica napus rapeseed; widely used globally)

gi_source.title: N/A — Canola oil contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Canola oil is a pure fat with 0g carbohydrate per 100g. Glycemic index is not applicable. GL per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (6.8g/100g); high monounsaturated fat (oleic acid ~63%); zero sodium
hypertension.guidance: Canola oil is recommended for hypertensive patients due to its lowest saturated fat content among common cooking oils, zero sodium, and high oleic acid content which supports LDL reduction of approximately 0.2–0.4 mmol/L compared to saturated fat-rich oils per published meta-analyses.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate; high MUFA (oleic acid) improves insulin sensitivity; omega-3 ALA (9.1%) contributes to metabolic health
type2_diabetes.guidance: Canola oil is suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, does not raise blood glucose, and its high monounsaturated fat content has been documented in multiple RCTs to improve insulin sensitivity and glycaemic control when substituted for saturated fat.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lowest saturated fat of common cooking oils; high MUFA; omega-3 ALA 9.1%; vitamin E 17.5mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Canola oil is cardioprotective: its fatty acid profile (6.8% saturated fat, 63% oleic acid, 9.1% ALA omega-3) supports LDL reduction and cardiovascular risk reduction, and HPB Singapore recommends it as the primary home cooking oil over palm oil, coconut oil, and lard.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 ALA (9.1%); anti-inflammatory fatty acid profile; vitamin E 17.5mg/100g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Canola oil provides plant-based omega-3 ALA (9.1%) which partially converts to EPA/DHA, contributing to reduced inflammatory load; its high vitamin E content (17.5mg/100g, highest of common cooking oils) provides additional antioxidant benefit for RA patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Canola oil (refined rapeseed oil) is a pale yellow, neutral-flavoured vegetable oil extracted from the seeds of Brassica napus, a genetically improved rapeseed variety developed in Canada in the 1970s. The term 'canola' distinguishes it from traditional high-erucic acid rapeseed; modern canola contains less than 2% erucic acid and negligible glucosinolates, making it safe for regular consumption. It is a pure fat providing 884 kcal per 100g with zero protein, zero carbohydrate, and zero sugar. Its fatty acid profile is the most favourable of any common cooking oil: lowest saturated fat at 6.8g/100g, high monounsaturated fat (oleic acid ~63%), meaningful plant omega-3 ALA at 9.1%, and the highest vitamin E content (17.5mg/100g as alpha-tocopherol) of any common cooking oil. It has a smoke point of approximately 204°C, making it suitable for wok stir-frying, sautéing, baking, and general home cooking. HPB Singapore explicitly recommends canola oil as the primary home cooking oil over palm oil, coconut oil, and lard. Clinically, it is suitable for all three major chronic conditions: it does not raise blood glucose (zero carbohydrate), supports LDL reduction and cardiovascular health through its MUFA and omega-3 content, and its low saturated fat profile benefits hypertensive and cardiovascular patients. The omega-3 ALA in canola is the plant-form precursor that partially converts to EPA/DHA at approximately 5–10% efficiency — not a substitute for fish oil, but a meaningful daily contribution for those using canola as their primary cooking fat.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy for canola oil — an exact match to the food. Values are consistent with the input record and cross-validated against known canola oil composition. HPB Singapore recommendation confirmed.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as canola oil is a pure refined fat containing no protein. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as canola oil contains no carbohydrate. Confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. fat_g is 100g/100g as this is a pure oil. glycemic_index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g carbohydrate per 100g); gi_category set to not_applicable. trans_fat_g reported as 0.4g/100g per USDA SR Legacy (naturally occurring trans fats from plant sources, not industrial trans fats). monounsaturated_fat_g (63.3g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (28.1g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172336. All micronutrient fields reported as 0.0 are confirmed zero or below detection threshold per USDA SR Legacy for refined canola oil, with the exception of vitamin_e_mg (17.5mg/100g) which is the primary micronutrient of note. Reference serving is 1 tablespoon = 14g per HPB Singapore and standard culinary convention.
